search_queries_used: site:entnet.org BPPV update reassessment persistent symptoms education fall risk 2017
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic Dix-Hallpike interpretation, repositioning manoeuvre, vestibular suppressant, imaging, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Diagnosis and recurrence require clinician-observed findings and differential assessment.
No procedure, medicine, investigation, referral, or treatment instruction is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults aged 18 years or older with clinician-established or suspected BPPV follow-up; children and central or non-positional vertigo require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, ENT, audiovestibular, physiotherapy, or neurology follow-up.
UAE_applicability: International specialty guidance requires UAE vestibular service, fall-risk, rehabilitation, emergency, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official AAO-HNSF 2017 BPPV update exact action statements were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2017 update remains the current AAO-HNSF BPPV guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact BPPV assessment section supports positional symptom context, interval change, functional impact, associated or negative features, clinician-performed assessment, and fall-risk modifiers without asserting persistent or recurrent BPPV.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting positional symptom context, clinician-performed diagnostic assessment, mobility, central nervous system context, home support, fall risk, and factors affecting management without asserting BPPV.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact reassessment section supports concerns, clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without selecting a repositioning manoeuvre or medication.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led outcome reassessment, persistent symptom documentation, safety discussion, questions, and arranged follow-up without generating treatment or a manoeuvre.

unresolved_source_gaps: Positional trigger, episode duration, nystagmus, side, manoeuvre, response, falls, neurological symptoms, gait, examination, diagnosis, treatment, rehabilitation, referral, and timing remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
